case_name: Oberholster   v Little & Anor [2020] EWHC 2635 (QB) (06 October 2020)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: The underlying claim relates to elective ophthalmic surgery (refractive lens exchange). The Claimant ('C') contracted with the optometrist Optical Express ('D2') to undertake the surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: C alleged not that the operation had been performed negligently, but that prior to the operation there was a negligent failure to obtain informed consent for the ophthalmic surgery due to a failure to discuss risks/adverse outcomes and misrepresenting risks.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Judge ruled that there was no abuse of process in C seeking to resolve costs against D8. She was not obliged to discontinue.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: He adopted the submission that 'the expert evidence confirms that ultimate responsibility for consent lies with the surgeon, D8.'
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The Judge was entitled to come to the conclusion in a summary process that it was difficult to envisage circumstances in which D2 would be liable for want of consent, but D8 would not be liable.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The decisions of the Judge were well within the generous ambit of discretion available to a Judge deciding costs issues.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Consent Process Review
requestables.description: Review of the consent process for refractive lens exchange surgery

reveal.result_summary: Consent process involved initial consultations with optometrists and sales' people, and provision of a consent form by D2 for C to read.
reveal.clinical_significance: Failure to adequately discuss risks and adverse outcomes.
reveal.decision_impact: Informed consent was not properly obtained.

timeline_phases.name: Preoperative Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes preoperative assessment and consent process

key_events.event: Initial consultations with optometrists and sales' people

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient scheduled for elective ophthalmic surgery

options.description: Ensure comprehensive discussion of risks and alternatives
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to ensure patient is fully informed.

options.description: Proceed with minimal discussion of risks
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate disclosure of risks and alternatives.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with minimal discussion of risks
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant relied on the process of D2.

expected_action_court.description: Comprehensive discussion of risks and alternatives should have been ensured
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence confirms ultimate responsibility for consent lies with the surgeon.

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on evidence E002 and E004
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Comprehensive consent process should have been followed.

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to obtain informed consent
Responsibility for consent lies with the surgeon

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper consent process would have informed the patient of risks

factual_timeline.event: Initial consultations with optometrists and sales' people

court_findings.legal_significance: Ultimate responsibility for consent lies with the surgeon
